claim: Petrodollar recycling erosion is a meaningful structural variable connecting dollar dynamics to the correlation regime through declining price-insensitive Treasury demand
needs: Quantitative decomposition isolating petrodollar recycling impact from larger forces of inflation expectations and monetary policy; has been widely discussed since 2015 without empirical confirmation of magnitude; causal chain requires several intermediate steps

claim: USD/JPY encodes global duration risk appetite and serves as a 1-3 month early-warning system for correlation regime shifts via carry unwind signaling
needs: Multiple confirming observations beyond October 2023 episode; filtering mechanism to reduce false positives from BOJ intervention, idiosyncratic Japanese macro; corroboration from other agents

claim: Phillips Curve convexity near the zero output gap makes the current economy maximally sensitive to small shocks producing 0.15-0.25 correlation swings in rolling 3-month windows
needs: Empirical validation at this specific output gap level; confirmation that the convexity relationship holds across different inflation regimes; larger sample of near-zero output gap observations

claim: The NAIRU has shifted up 0.3-0.5pp to ~4.3-4.5% meaning current 4.1% unemployment is tighter than it appears
needs: Real-time NAIRU estimates are notoriously imprecise (±1pp confidence bands per Staiger-Stock-Watson 1997); shift could reverse with immigration policy changes or participation recovery; needs sustained wage data confirmation

claim: The Taiwan contingency is the single largest underpriced tail risk for rates-equity correlation given 90% semiconductor disruption and potential Chinese Treasury liquidation (~$800B)
needs: Probability assessment is inherently uncertain (intelligence estimates range 5-35%); the claim that it is underpriced requires more granular options market evidence; correlation mechanics conditional on the event are high confidence but probability-weighted assessment is speculative

claim: Geopolitical fragmentation (defense spending, tech decoupling, reshoring) is a substantial independent driver of positive correlation beyond the standard inflation and fiscal channels
needs: Empirical isolation of geopolitical fragmentation effect from standard fiscal/inflation channels; may be better understood as a political economy explanation for why fiscal consolidation is unavailable rather than an independent driver

claim: The BOJ YCC abandonment provides a natural experiment suggesting transmission restoration initially promotes positive correlation with a 12-18 month lag
needs: N=1 observation from Japan; institutional context (decades of deflation, yen depreciation channel, different household portfolios) limits generalizability; directional insight needs confirmation from additional cases of transmission restoration

claim: Unstable oscillation may constitute a permanent third regime rather than a transition between positive and negative correlation regimes
needs: Two within-year reversals do not distinguish between stable positive regime with temporary shocks, regime transition in progress, or permanent instability; expensive to position for if correlation stabilizes; requires multi-year evidence and theoretical foundations for a stable third equilibrium
